title: World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France sit atop the 2026 World Cup winner market as co-favorites due to their blend of recent major-tournament success and exceptional squad depth. Spain’s Euro 2024 triumph highlighted a dynamic, youthful attack featuring talents like Lamine Yamal, while France’s roster offers unmatched versatility across positions under Didier Deschamps. England’s strong club form and emerging depth keep them within striking distance, creating a tight cluster at the top. South American powers Argentina and Brazil maintain realistic paths through experience and attacking quality, but the expanded 48-team field and balanced European depth sustain competitive pricing among the leading contenders heading into the June 11 kickoff.
